title: Section 8 Apartments for Rent in Cleveland Heights, OH
metaTitle: Section 8 Apartments for Rent in Cleveland Heights, OH
metaDescription: Looking for Section 8 apartments for rent in Cleveland Heights, OH? See how CMHA vouchers, the local SOI ordinance, and nearby rentals work. Book a showing.
primaryKeyword: section 8 apartments for rent Cleveland Heights ohio
secondaryKeywords: voucher friendly apartments near university circle
answerBox: Cleveland Heights, a Cuyahoga County suburb bordering University Circle, is one of the few local suburbs with a 2021 ordinance protecting Housing Choice Voucher holders from source-of-income discrimination. CMHA administers Section 8 countywide. Rent Finder Cleveland doesn't yet manage units inside Cleveland Heights, but manages voucher-friendly homes nearby in Glenville and Hough.

sections.h2: Can I rent a Section 8 apartment in Cleveland Heights?
body: Yes, in practical terms: Cleveland Heights adopted a source-of-income protection ordinance in 2021, meaning a landlord there generally cannot turn away an applicant solely for paying with a Housing Choice Voucher. Rent Finder Cleveland does not currently manage apartment units inside Cleveland Heights itself, though. Our 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes sit mostly on the City of Cleveland's east and southeast side, and the closest of those — **Glenville** (ZIP 44108) and **Hough** (ZIP 44103) — border Cleveland Heights along the University Circle line.

If a Cleveland Heights address specifically matters to your search, [book a free showing](/p/book-showing) and tell our leasing team your criteria. We'll flag it if that changes and point you toward the nearest current availability in the meantime.

sections.h2: How a Housing Choice Voucher works here
body: Housing Choice Vouchers for Cleveland Heights and the rest of Cuyahoga County are issued and administered by [CMHA](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) (the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority), not by the suburb itself. CMHA's preliminary application stays open online year-round with no closing date, and applicants are chosen from that list through periodic random "mini-lotteries" rather than in strict order — CMHA's own guidance says a name might come up within days, or it may take months. Applying requires an active email address, Social Security numbers and birth dates for everyone in the household, and household income figures.

Once a household holds a voucher, the specific unit still has to clear a housing-quality inspection before assistance is paid. HUD is phasing out the older HQS standard in favor of NSPIRE, with a final transition deadline of February 1, 2027 for the [Housing Choice Voucher program](https://www.hud.gov/helping-americans/housing-choice-vouchers). Our [income-limit guide](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/section-8-income-limits-cuyahoga-county/) covers the current CMHA thresholds in more detail.

sections.h2: Cleveland Heights' voucher protection, compared with Cleveland
body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring a landlord to accept a Housing Choice Voucher, and the City of Cleveland has no ordinance requiring it either — Cleveland's 2024 "Pay to Stay" law dropped its source-of-income provisions during negotiation. Cleveland Heights took a different path: reporting describes the suburb adopting its own source-of-income ordinance in 2021, one of a small handful of Cuyahoga County communities — also reportedly including South Euclid, University Heights, and Warrensville Heights — that generally bar refusing an applicant solely for using a voucher.

Confirm current protections directly with the city before relying on this, since municipal ordinances can change. Our [source-of-income guide](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/source-of-income-discrimination-cleveland-ohio/) walks through the jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction picture across Greater Cleveland in more detail.

sections.h2: Getting to and around Cleveland Heights
body: Cleveland Heights has no rail station of its own, but it sits directly between two RTA rail corridors. The [Blue and Green light-rail lines](https://www.riderta.com/routes) run through neighboring Shaker Heights just south of the suburb, and the Red Line's Windermere terminus is a short bus ride away in East Cleveland to the north. The HealthLine, RTA's bus-rapid-transit route on Euclid Avenue, links Cleveland Heights-area bus routes directly to University Circle and on to Downtown's Tower City rail hub, the point where all four rail lines converge.

Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages — currently concentrated in Cleveland's Glenville, Hough, Slavic Village, Collinwood, Fairfax/Central, and Buckeye-Shaker neighborhoods — accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and stays HUD-inspection-ready. Glenville and Hough sit closest to Cleveland Heights, both bordering University Circle just to the west, so those are typically where we'd point a Cleveland Heights-focused search first.

[Book a showing](/p/book-showing) to see current availability near Cleveland Heights, or read our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full local application walkthrough, from CMHA's preliminary list through move-in.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage Section 8 apartments in Cleveland Heights?
faq.a: Not currently. Our 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated in the City of Cleveland, with the closest current availability typically in Glenville (44108) and Hough (44103), both bordering Cleveland Heights near University Circle. Tell our leasing team what you're looking for and we'll point you to the nearest fit.

faq.q: Does Cleveland Heights protect Section 8 voucher holders?
faq.a: Reporting indicates Cleveland Heights adopted a source-of-income protection ordinance in 2021, meaning a landlord there generally cannot refuse an applicant solely for using a Housing Choice Voucher. That's different from the City of Cleveland, which currently has no such ordinance. Confirm current rules with the city before relying on this.

faq.q: Who administers Section 8 vouchers for Cleveland Heights?
faq.a: CMHA, the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority, administers Housing Choice Vouchers for the whole county, including Cleveland Heights. Applications run through CMHA's online portal year-round, and selection happens through periodic random mini-lotteries rather than strict first-come-first-served order.

faq.q: What's the income limit for a Section 8 voucher near Cleveland Heights?
faq.a: Per CMHA's 2025 figures, income limits run roughly $34,800 for one person, $39,800 for two, $49,700 for four, and $65,650 for eight, and these are updated periodically. Check CMHA's current chart before applying, since these numbers change.

faq.q: How do I get from Cleveland Heights to University Circle without a car?
faq.a: RTA's HealthLine bus-rapid-transit route on Euclid Avenue connects Cleveland Heights-area buses directly to University Circle and Downtown's Tower City. The Blue and Green light-rail lines through neighboring Shaker Heights, and the Red Line's Windermere station in East Cleveland, offer additional rail connections nearby.
